blurb: The fullness of time arrived not with fanfare but with the quiet entrance of vulnerability—God wrapped in flesh, born under the very law that held us captive. What seemed like cosmic delay was actually perfect timing: the moment when heaven's rescue could be understood in the language of cradles and tears. We who were once slaves to our own inadequacy have been adopted as children, and the Spirit within us now cries out with the confidence of beloved sons and daughters. The inheritance was always meant to be ours; we simply needed to learn the family name.

verses.text: But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son,
To redeem them that were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of sons.
And because ye are God’s sons, He hath sent forth his Spirit into our hearts, crying, Abba, Father
Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
